evals.prompt: I want to add BarcodeCapture to my Android app. Here's my empty Activity: EmptyActivity.kt. I need to scan EAN-13 and Code 128 barcodes for a retail app.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads integration.md, fetches the latest SDK version, writes complete BarcodeCapture integration code into EmptyActivity.kt (BarcodeCapture.forDataCaptureContext, BarcodeCaptureListener, Camera, DataCaptureView, BarcodeCaptureOverlay, onResume/onPause lifecycle), and shows the setup checklist (Gradle deps, AndroidManifest permissions, runtime permission, license key).

assertions.text: Setup checklist is shown and mentions adding com.scandit.datacapture:barcode and com.scandit.datacapture:core Gradle dependencies
Setup checklist mentions adding CAMERA permission to AndroidManifest.xml
Setup checklist mentions requesting CAMERA permission at runtime
A license key placeholder string is present
DataCaptureContext.forLicenseKey( is used to create the context
BarcodeCapture.createRecommendedCameraSettings() is used (not VideoResolution.AUTO)
Camera.getDefaultCamera( is used to obtain the camera
dataCaptureContext.setFrameSource( is called with the camera
BarcodeCaptureSettings is constructed and configured
Symbology.EAN13_UPCA is enabled
Symbology.CODE128 is enabled
No symbologies other than EAN13_UPCA and CODE128 are enabled
BarcodeCapture.forDataCaptureContext(dataCaptureContext, settings) is used to create the mode
barcodeCapture.addListener( is called
DataCaptureView.newInstance( is used to create the view
BarcodeCaptureOverlay.newInstance(barcodeCapture, dataCaptureView) is called
The Activity implements BarcodeCaptureListener
onBarcodeScanned is overridden and reads session.newlyRecognizedBarcode
onSessionUpdated, onObservationStarted, and onObservationStopped are overridden
barcodeCapture.isEnabled = false is called inside onBarcodeScanned
camera.switchToDesiredState(FrameSourceState.ON) is called in onResume (or equivalent)
camera.switchToDesiredState(FrameSourceState.OFF) is called in onPause (or equivalent)
barcodeCapture.removeListener( is called in onDestroy
dataCaptureContext.removeCurrentMode() is called in onDestroy

evals.prompt: Add barcode scanning to my existing Android Activity. Here's the Activity: EmptyActivity.kt. I want to scan QR codes and Data Matrix. When a barcode is scanned, print the barcode data to the log.
evals.expected_output: The skill integrates BarcodeCapture into EmptyActivity.kt with QR and Data Matrix symbologies enabled. onBarcodeScanned logs the barcode data using Log.d or similar. Shows the setup checklist.

assertions.text: Symbology.QR is enabled
Symbology.DATA_MATRIX is enabled
No symbologies other than QR and DATA_MATRIX are enabled
BarcodeCapture.forDataCaptureContext( is used
onBarcodeScanned is implemented and accesses barcode.data
The scan result is logged (Log.d, println, or similar)
onResume turns the camera on
onPause turns the camera off
Setup checklist mentions Gradle dependencies

evals.prompt: When a barcode is scanned in my Android BarcodeCapture app, I want to disable scanning, look up the barcode in a remote API (a suspend function / coroutine call), and re-enable scanning when the lookup completes. Wire this up safely.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets barcodeCapture.isEnabled = false at the start of onBarcodeScanned, launches a coroutine for the async lookup, and re-enables scanning when the coroutine completes. Uses lifecycleScope or similar so the coroutine is tied to the Activity lifecycle.

assertions.text: barcodeCapture.isEnabled = false is set inside onBarcodeScanned before the async work
A coroutine (launch, async) or runOnUiThread with post is used to perform the async lookup
barcodeCapture.isEnabled = true is set after the lookup completes
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: I want to suppress the default beep and vibration when a barcode is scanned in my Android app — silent scanning. Update the BarcodeCapture feedback.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets barcodeCapture.feedback to a BarcodeCaptureFeedback with no sound or vibration (either the empty constructor or by assigning null/empty Feedback to the success property).

assertions.text: barcodeCapture.feedback is reassigned to suppress audio and haptic feedback (BarcodeCaptureFeedback() empty constructor or equivalent)
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: Add a square viewfinder to my Android BarcodeCapture preview so users can see where to aim.
evals.expected_output: The skill creates a RectangularViewfinder with SQUARE style and assigns it to overlay.viewfinder. The overlay is the BarcodeCaptureOverlay created in the Activity.

assertions.text: A RectangularViewfinder is constructed with a SQUARE style (or equivalent)
The viewfinder is assigned to a BarcodeCaptureOverlay's viewfinder property (overlay.viewfinder = ...)
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: I need to filter scans so duplicates of the same code within 500 milliseconds are ignored. Update my BarcodeCapture settings.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets codeDuplicateFilter = TimeInterval.millis(500) on the BarcodeCaptureSettings instance before creating the BarcodeCapture mode (or applies new settings via applySettings).

assertions.text: codeDuplicateFilter is set to TimeInterval.millis(500) on the BarcodeCaptureSettings (not a bare integer 500)
codeDuplicateFilter is set before BarcodeCapture.forDataCaptureContext( or via applySettings
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: Clean up my BarcodeCapture Android integration properly when the Activity is destroyed — remove the listener, remove the mode from the context, and turn off the camera.
evals.expected_output: The skill ensures onDestroy calls barcodeCapture.removeListener(this) and dataCaptureContext.removeCurrentMode(), and that onPause calls camera.switchToDesiredState(FrameSourceState.OFF).

assertions.text: onDestroy calls barcodeCapture.removeListener(this) or equivalent
onDestroy calls dataCaptureContext.removeCurrentMode() or removeAllModes()
onPause (or onDestroy) calls camera.switchToDesiredState(FrameSourceState.OFF)
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: Add an aimer-style viewfinder (a small crosshair with the Scandit logo) to my Android BarcodeCapture preview so users know where to point the camera.
evals.expected_output: The skill constructs an AimerViewfinder() and assigns it to the BarcodeCaptureOverlay's viewfinder property (overlay.viewfinder). Existing integration is preserved.

assertions.text: AimerViewfinder() is constructed with no arguments
The viewfinder is assigned to the overlay via overlay.viewfinder =
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: I want a horizontal laser-line viewfinder on my Android BarcodeCapture preview instead of a rectangle. Add it.
evals.expected_output: The skill constructs a LaserlineViewfinder() and assigns it to the BarcodeCaptureOverlay's viewfinder property (overlay.viewfinder). Existing integration is preserved.

assertions.text: LaserlineViewfinder() is constructed with no arguments
The viewfinder is assigned to the overlay via overlay.viewfinder =
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: Restrict barcode scanning to a rectangular region in the center of the preview on my Android BarcodeCapture app — about 90% of the view width and 30% of the height — so codes outside that band are ignored.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets settings.locationSelection to a RectangularLocationSelection created via the withSize factory, using a SizeWithUnit built from DoubleWithUnit values with MeasureUnit.FRACTION. The location selection is set on BarcodeCaptureSettings before the mode is created (or applied via applySettings). Existing integration is preserved.

assertions.text: settings.locationSelection is assigned a location selection instance
RectangularLocationSelection.withSize( is used (not a direct constructor)
SizeWithUnit is constructed for the rectangle dimensions
DoubleWithUnit is used with MeasureUnit.FRACTION for the fractional dimensions
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: Reject scans that don't start with the prefix "ACME-" in my Android BarcodeCapture app. When a non-matching code is scanned, don't act on it and keep scanning; only accept codes with that prefix.
evals.expected_output: Inside onBarcodeScanned the skill reads barcode.data, checks barcode.data.startsWith("ACME-"), and returns early (continuing to scan) for non-matching codes while only handling and disabling scanning for accepted codes. There is no built-in reject API; rejection is done in the listener.

assertions.text: onBarcodeScanned reads the scanned value from session.newlyRecognizedBarcode
barcode.data is compared with startsWith("ACME-") to decide acceptance
The listener returns early without acting on a rejected code so scanning continues
barcodeCapture.isEnabled = false is set only for an accepted code
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: Change the highlight color of recognized barcodes in my Android BarcodeCapture app to a semi-transparent green fill with a solid green outline.
evals.expected_output: The skill assigns a Brush to overlay.brush built with Brush(fillColor, strokeColor, strokeWidth) using android.graphics.Color values (e.g. Color.argb for the fill and Color.rgb for the stroke). The brush property is assigned, not set via a method. Existing integration is preserved.

assertions.text: A Brush is constructed with Brush(fillColor, strokeColor, strokeWidth)
android.graphics.Color values are used for the fill and stroke colors
The brush is assigned to the overlay via overlay.brush =
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: Enable Code 39 full ASCII decoding in my Android BarcodeCapture app so lowercase and control characters encoded in Code 39 are read.
evals.expected_output: The skill gets the Code 39 SymbologySettings via settings.getSymbologySettings(Symbology.CODE39) and calls setExtensionEnabled("full_ascii", true) on it. Existing integration is preserved.

assertions.text: settings.getSymbologySettings(Symbology.CODE39) is used to get the per-symbology settings
setExtensionEnabled("full_ascii", true) is called on the Code 39 SymbologySettings
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: My Code 39 barcodes use a mod 43 checksum. Configure the Android BarcodeCapture settings so Code 39 codes are validated with the mod 43 checksum.
evals.expected_output: The skill gets the Code 39 SymbologySettings via settings.getSymbologySettings(Symbology.CODE39) and sets its checksums property to a set containing Checksum.MOD_43 (e.g. EnumSet.of(Checksum.MOD_43)). Existing integration is preserved.

assertions.text: settings.getSymbologySettings(Symbology.CODE39) is used to get the per-symbology settings
The checksums property is assigned a set containing Checksum.MOD_43
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: I need to scan Code 128 barcodes that are longer than the default supported length in my Android BarcodeCapture app. Widen the active symbol counts for Code 128.
evals.expected_output: The skill gets the Code 128 SymbologySettings via settings.getSymbologySettings(Symbology.CODE128) and sets its activeSymbolCounts property to a Set<Int> covering the needed range (e.g. setOf(6, 7, ..., 40)). Existing integration is preserved.

assertions.text: settings.getSymbologySettings(Symbology.CODE128) is used to get the per-symbology settings
The activeSymbolCounts property is assigned a Set of Int values
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: Some of my Code 128 labels are printed light-on-dark (color inverted). Enable color-inverted decoding for Code 128 in my Android BarcodeCapture app.
evals.expected_output: The skill gets the Code 128 SymbologySettings via settings.getSymbologySettings(Symbology.CODE128) and sets isColorInvertedEnabled = true on it, keeping the symbology enabled. Existing integration is preserved.

assertions.text: settings.getSymbologySettings(Symbology.CODE128) is used to get the per-symbology settings
isColorInvertedEnabled = true is set on the Code 128 SymbologySettings
Symbology.CODE128 remains enabled
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: I need to scan GS1 composite codes (a linear barcode with a 2D companion) in my Android BarcodeCapture app. Enable composite code scanning for composite types A, B, and C.
evals.expected_output: The skill enables the symbologies for the composite types via settings.enableSymbologies(compositeTypes) (Kotlin overload taking an EnumSet<CompositeType>) AND sets settings.enabledCompositeTypes to the same composite types (CompositeType.A, CompositeType.B, CompositeType.C). Existing integration is preserved.

assertions.text: settings.enableSymbologies( is called with an EnumSet of CompositeType values to enable the composite component symbologies
settings.enabledCompositeTypes is assigned the composite types
CompositeType.A, CompositeType.B, and CompositeType.C are referenced
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, listener) is preserved
